title: Fix founder/admin recognition so the authenticated user can access the Admin Dashboard

requirements.text: In the backend (main.mo), ensure the admin/founder principal is reliably stored at canister initialization time (using the deployer's principal). Expose a stable query `isAdmin() : async Bool` that returns `true` only when the caller's principal matches the stored admin principal. Ensure this function works correctly after canister upgrades and does not reset the stored admin principal.

quotes: i cannot login as a founder and notify that im a founder so i can open the dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: The admin principal is captured from the deployer at first initialization and persisted in stable storage.
Calling isAdmin() as the deployer principal returns true.
Calling isAdmin() as any other principal returns false.
The admin principal is NOT reset on canister upgrade.

requirements.text: In frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, fix the `useIsAdmin` React Query hook so it correctly calls the backend `isAdmin()` query and returns the boolean result. Ensure the hook re-fetches when the authenticated actor changes and that errors are handled gracefully (defaulting to false).

quotes: i cannot login as a founder and notify that im a founder so i can open the dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: useIsAdmin returns true when the authenticated user is the deployer/admin principal.
useIsAdmin returns false for non-admin users.
The hook refetches automatically when the actor (identity) changes.
On error, the hook defaults to false without crashing.

requirements.text: In frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx, update the Admin Dashboard tab visibility logic to use the result of the `useIsAdmin` hook. The Admin tab should only be rendered and visible when `isAdmin` is true. Display a clear visual indicator (e.g., a badge or label) on the Admin tab so the founder can confirm they have admin access after logging in.

quotes: i cannot login as a founder and notify that im a founder so i can open the dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: The Admin Dashboard tab is visible in the tab list only when the authenticated user is the admin.
Non-admin authenticated users do not see the Admin Dashboard tab.
After logging in as the founder/deployer, the Admin tab appears without requiring a page reload.
The tab includes a visual cue (badge or distinct label) confirming admin access.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
The admin principal must be stored in stable variables so it survives canister upgrades.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo.
nonGoals: Adding additional admin roles or multi-admin support.
Changing the daily stats data model or getDailyStats endpoint behavior.
Modifying the AdminDashboard component UI beyond tab visibility.
